That's University Minnesota Health Occupational Therapist Megan Bresnahan other experts like describe condition.
When child sensory processing issues, brain have difficulty processing information provided child's senses.
This affect child's ability respond appropriately complete routine childhood activities, said Bresnahan, provides care Adoption Medicine Clinic University Minnesota Masonic Children's Hospital.
Children have experienced early deprivation such institutional care, neglect abuse those chaotic early life high risk develop sensory processing disorder.
Even children experience early adversity still face sensory challenges; sometimes these issues related other disorders syndromes.
matter cause, there many activities offer your child home help support their sensory needs.
structure routine.
This will help your child ready what will happen next during calming children with SPD.
Reduce clutter keep areas organized possible.
chaotic environment make child's sensory processing difficulties worse.
Provide regular opportunities sensory input activities.
Encourage your child crab walk, bear walk, wheelbarrow walk frog between daily activities.
These done morning between normal routines, like brushing teeth, getting dressed, going breakfast table, etc.
Before sitting down meals homework, encourage your child perform chair push-ups.
Allow your child play with draw pictures using shaving cream bathtub shower, outside.
Apply lotion your child's skin after bath time.
Have your child push cart when shopping, help carry groceries.
Both these activities provide heavy work.
Examples include: books, pots pans, gallon milk container filled with water.
Provide "sensory bins" play outlet your child.
bins filled with kinetic sand, moon sand, rice, beans, corn, noodles other similar material.
Serve chewy crunchy foods your child.
increased flavor your child's foods.
Provide apple sauce, smoothies runny yogurt your child consume through straw.
weighted blanket, snake buddy.
create these items filling long tube sock with popcorn kernels, kidney beans rice.
your child help with household chores such carrying moving laundry basket detergent, pushing laundry basket, vacuuming, digging garden.
Create calming area where your child have quiet time.
This spot contain rocking chair beanbag chair.
Make obstacle course house your child.
Play music that reflects mood energy level your child.
Enroll your child activities outside home that beneficial sensory processing, including: swimming, horseback riding, bike riding, hiking, gymnastics, karate yoga.
Originally posted Health Blog University Minnesota 2016.
